title: Direct Answer
description: Answer from model knowledge when no tool, search, file, or intermediate reasoning is needed.
when_to_use: The request can be answered without external state or tool access.
The user asks for a concise explanation, definition, rewrite, or summary of provided text.
skeleton: result: produce the final user-facing answer
adaptation_notes: Use a tool recipe instead if the request asks for current data, files, web search, memory, or calendar state.
Use conditional_branch when the request includes if/otherwise logic, even when the branch can be answered from stable knowledge.
Do not add think nodes for ordinary explanations; use result unless genuine multi-step reasoning is required.
Use a think node before the result for multi-premise logic puzzles or constraint-elimination tasks.
For vague requests, use a result node to ask one focused clarifying question or offer safe next steps; do not reject merely for vagueness.
For summary requests, answer as a summary, not as a recommendation about how to summarize.
